skill.name: developing-genkit-python
skill.description: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Python. Use when the user asks about Genkit, AI agents, flows, or tools in Python, or when encountering Genkit errors, import issues, or API problems.
skill.summary: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Python with flows, tools, and model integrations.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 233 potential issues, primarily false positives from markdown code formatting being misinterpreted as shell commands. All external_commands detections are backtick code spans in markdown documentation. Network/environment access patterns are standard AI framework configuration documentation (API key setup, localhost URLs). No malicious behavior, credential exfiltration, or command injection evidence found. Skill is legitimate Firebase Genkit Python documentation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged 204 instances of backtick-enclosed code spans as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. In markdown documentation, backticks are standard syntax for inline code formatting. No actual shell command execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged patterns are markdown code formatting syntax (backticks), not shell execution. Documentation skill naturally contains many code examples.

low_findings.title: Standard API Key Configuration Patterns
low_findings.description: Environment variable access for API keys (GOOGLE_API_KEY, etc.) is standard Genkit configuration. No evidence of credential exfiltration - these are documented setup patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key environment variables are required for AI framework functionality. No network exfiltration evidence found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs for localhost and genkit.dev are standard for developer documentation. No external data exfiltration endpoints detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are localhost references and official documentation links, expected in development guides.

content.user_title: Build AI agents and flows with Genkit in Python
content.value_statement: Developers building AI-powered applications with Genkit in Python face a learning curve with flows, tools, and model integrations. This skill provides practical guidance for setting up projects, implementing patterns, and troubleshooting common issues.
actual_capabilities: Set up Genkit projects with CLI initialization and configuration
Create AI flows with input schemas, output parsers, and step functions
Define and register custom tools for AI agent interaction
Configure model providers (Vertex AI, Google AI, OpenAI)
Implement prompts with Dotprompt template syntax
Debug and fix common Genkit errors and import issues
limitations: Does not provide AI model access - requires user to configure their own API keys
Cannot execute flows at runtime - provides guidance only
Does not replace official Genkit documentation for all edge cases

use_cases.title: Initialize a new Genkit project from scratch
use_cases.description: Set up a new Python Genkit project with proper directory structure, configuration files, and initial flow implementation.

use_cases.title: Debug import errors and runtime failures
use_cases.description: Identify and resolve common issues like missing packages, incorrect imports, or configuration problems in existing Genkit projects.

use_cases.title: Implement multi-step AI workflows
use_cases.description: Create sophisticated AI workflows with multiple steps, tool calls, and conditional logic using Genkit flows.
use_cases.target_user: Experienced developers building complex AI features

prompt_templates.title: Setup Genkit project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new Genkit Python project. I want to create a flow that [describe your use case]. What packages do I need and how should I configure my model provider?

prompt_templates.title: Debug Genkit errors
prompt_templates.prompt: I am getting this error in my Genkit project: [paste error message]. My setup includes [describe your setup]. How do I fix it?

prompt_templates.title: Create AI flow with tools
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a Genkit flow that [describe workflow]. How do I define custom tools and connect them to my flow in Python?

prompt_templates.title: Configure multiple model providers
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure my Genkit project to use [Vertex AI / Google AI / OpenAI] as my model provider? Show me the configuration and authentication steps.

output_examples.input: How do I create a simple Genkit flow that takes a user question and returns an AI-generated answer?
output_examples.output: A flow definition with an input schema for the question, a step function that calls the model, and an output parser for the response. Shows the directory structure and key configuration steps.

output_examples.input: I'm getting 'ModuleNotFoundError: genkit' when trying to run my flow
output_examples.output: Troubleshooting steps: verify package installation with pip list, check Python environment, suggest using virtual environments, and confirm correct import statements.

best_practices: Use virtual environments to isolate Genkit dependencies from system packages
Define clear input/output schemas for flows to ensure type safety and validation
Store API keys in environment variables, never hardcode them in source files
anti_patterns: Do not skip schema definitions - flows without schemas are harder to test and debug
Avoid hardcoding model names or API endpoints - use configuration files
Do not ignore import errors - they often indicate missing dependencies or version mismatches

faq.question: What Python version is required for Genkit?
faq.answer: Genkit Python requires Python 3.10 or later. Check your version with 'python --version'.

faq.question: How do I configure API keys for model providers?
faq.answer: Set environment variables like GOOGLE_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY in your .env file. Never commit these to version control.

faq.question: Can Genkit run without an internet connection?
faq.answer: Basic flow definitions work offline, but model calls require network access to AI providers.

faq.question: How do I debug a flow that is not producing expected output?
faq.answer: Add logging to your step functions, check the trace viewer in the Dev UI, and verify your input/output schemas match.

faq.question: What is the difference between a flow and a tool in Genkit?
faq.answer: Flows are multi-step workflows that orchestrate AI interactions. Tools are individual capabilities that AI models can call during generation.

faq.question: How do I add a custom tool to my Genkit flow?
faq.answer: Define a function with the @genkit.tool() decorator, register it with your flow, and it becomes available to the AI model during execution.
